title: Daily Signal Brief 2026-05-18 — Colorado AI Act paused; SAP and Workday ship agents
summary: Four signals elevated, top alert L3. (1) Colorado's AI Act paused by federal court Apr 27 and rewritten by the legislature May 9 (SB 26-189) — effective date pushed to 2027-01-01. Combined with the EU Omnibus deferral and Texas TRAIGA's intent-only standard, the statutory regime for AI hiring is materially looser for 18 months; Mobley v. Workday case law is now the binding constraint. (2) SAP Sapphire 2026 (May 12-13) unveiled the Autonomous Suite — 50+ Joule Assistants, 200+ agents across HR/finance/SCM/procurement/CX, with Nvidia and Anthropic named as allies. Workday brought its Sana Self-Service Agent into Microsoft 365 Copilot May 13 with 300+ HR/finance skills. Friday's refresh missed both; this brief recovers them. Action: Forge/Compass reframe positioning around 'distribution surface' not 'MCP-native'; Helm/Vector reset the compliance narrative around case law not statute. (3) Anthropic overtakes OpenAI in enterprise spend share for the first time per Ramp May 2026 AI Index (34.4% vs 32.3%) — Stratum's Claude bet looks more validated; sales no longer needs to defend foundation choice. (4) Mobley v. Workday re-elevated — March 6 ADEA ruling and March 30 amended complaint make the SaaS-vendor liability surface the most actionable AI-hiring risk for the next 18 months.
